title: Arrhenatherum macrostachyum
taxonomy: Arrhenatherum macrostachyum is classified as a perennial grass within the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Poeae and is native to the Mediterranean region. The genus Arrhenatherum is a monotypic genus, containing only one species, A. macrostachyum. This species is further divided into several subspecies, which exhibit varying characteristics and distributions. Arrhenatherum macrostachyum is also known as false oat-grass due to its similar appearance to the genus Avena.

intro: Arrhenatherum macrostachyum, commonly known as meadow oat-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, such as wound healing and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Arrhenatherum macrostachyum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and skin irritations, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
